meaning: adj.adv.张口发呆地,目瞪口呆地
examples: She stood looking at Carmen with her mouth agape.
He thought that God was agape. For him, that was the basis of Christianity. But Eros thought of the matter what St.Augustine said is selfish.
The only thing that would be agape.
The woman laid in bed all day long with an agape expression on her face.
After she uttered those words my mouth was agape for the rest of the panel.
